Use_case: Visium ST processing, visualization, image alignment & region annotation
Strengths: Extends Seurat; QC, deconvolution, digital unrolling, manual/landmark alignment, neighbourhood-enrichment & label-assortativity tests
Limitations: ST single-omics (Visium-focused); a toolkit rather than a multi-omics integrator
Best_for: Visium analysis, multi-section alignment, spatial statistics

Use_case: Co-register ST + metabolomics (MSI) + morphology from same / adjacent sections
Omics_layers: Visium ST + MALDI/DESI mass-spectrometry imaging + H&E
Strengths: Modular, accessible workflow; multi-tissue; supports MALDI & DESI; refined resolution & interpretability
Limitations: Preprint (2025), not yet peer-reviewed; co-registration / alignment focused
Best_for: MSI + Visium spatial multi-omics (e.g. tissue injury / pharmacology)
Study_examples: Drug-induced pulmonary fibrosis (in vivo); data GSE312168

Use_case: DL integration of spatial + omics layers
Strengths: Designed for spatial multi-omics; flexible integration
Limitations: Emerging tool; fewer benchmarks; needs compute expertise

Use_case: Spatial domains from expression + space + histology
Spatial multi-omics integration; spatial domain identification; multi-section integration (SMART-MS variant)
Omics_layers: Spatial transcriptomics + chromatin accessibility (ATAC) / proteomics / ribosomics / CUT&Tag
Technologies: 10x Visium, MISAR-seq, Stereo-CITE-seq, spatial ATAC-RNA-seq, spatial CUT&Tag-RNA-seq, STARmap + RIBOmap, SPOTS
Strengths: Modality-independent and modular; supports any number/type of omics; exceptional scalability on large datasets; SMART-MS variant for multi-section integration; triplet metric learning refines cross-modal aggregation
Limitations: Python-only with non-trivial installation (PyTorch Geometric wheel dependencies); published March 2026, limited independent benchmarking so far; requires CUDA for efficiency on large datasets
Best_for: Spatial domain identification across diverse multi-omics combinations; large-scale spatial datasets; multi-section spatial integration
Study_examples: 10x human lymph node (GSE263617); MISAR-seq mouse brain (OEP003285); spatial CUT&Tag-RNA-seq mouse brain (GSE205055); SPOTS mouse spleen (GSE198353); P22 mouse brain; 10x human tonsil
Benchmarked_in:
10.1038/s41467-026-70821-5
Internal benchmarks vs STAGATE, GraphST, SpaGCN, STAligner, BANKSY, PASTE2 (benchmarks/ folder in repo)
Preprocessing_steps: Expects pre-processed AnnData objects; MNN-based batch correction available via smart.MNN for multi-section data
Input_details: Spatial multi-omics AnnData objects with spatial coordinates; multiple omics layers as separate count matrices within AnnData
Output_formats: Integrated low-dimensional embeddings; spatial domain labels stored in AnnData .obs
Underlying_method: GraphSAGE (graph neural network) + triplet metric learning; modality-independent modular stacking framework

Use_case: Unsupervised temporal/spatial multi-omics integration; latent factor discovery
Strengths: Models continuous covariates (space coordinates); captures smooth trajectories; interpretable latent factors
Limitations: Requires continuous covariates; assumes smooth variation
Input_formats: Multi-view cell × feature matrices + continuous covariates
Input_details: Paired cells across omics layers; temporal, spatial or pseudotime coordinates
Output_formats: Latent factors, feature loadings, variance explained, spatial trajectories, uncertainty estimates
Underlying_method: Bayesian group factor analysis + Gaussian processes
